reason: @Injectable() + extends AuthGuard('jwt') generate IIFE branches in compiled JS that c8/Istanbul marks uncovered — architecturally unreachable in NestJS unit tests. Real conditional branches (missing authHeader, non-Bearer) are fully tested. Branch ceiling ~50%.

@Injectable() + PassportStrategy(Strategy) IIFE + super() constructor call produce decorator branches unreachable in unit tests. Branch ceiling ~69%.

TypeScript interface property lines (iss: string, name?: string at lines 9, 14-16) are source-map offset artifacts in c8 — not real uncovered logic. All service methods (loginWithGoogle, updateLastLogin, logAuthEvent) have full test coverage. Branch ceiling ~65%.
@Injectable() decorator IIFE + class field initializer artifacts. Remaining uncovered branches are in error-path combinations already covered by integration. Branch ceiling ~85% (closest to target).

description: generateRegistrationOptions, listPasskeys, deletePasskey lacked tenantId in Prisma queries — OWASP A01:2025 tenant isolation violation.
fix: Added tenantId param to all 3 methods; switched user.findUnique to user.findFirst with {id, tenantId}; added user:{tenantId} nested relation filter for passkey queries; threaded tenantId from controller via @CurrentUser.

description: passkey.findFirst({ where: { credentialId } }) without tenantId at line 155 — WebAuthn protocol necessity. Credential IDs are globally unique by design (64 random bytes). Tenant verification happens post-lookup at line 168 via passkey.user.tenant?.isActive.

reason: Class field initializer (private readonly logger = new Logger(...)) + empty constructor () {} produce c8 source-map artifacts. All real logic branches (sendOtp, verifyOtp paths) fully covered. Branch ceiling ~64%.
@Injectable() + class field initializers produce decorator IIFE artifacts. Real logic branches fully tested. Branch ceiling ~79%.
